name: pydocstyle
documentation:
# pydocstyle - docstring style checker

```{image} https://github.com/PyCQA/pydocstyle/workflows/Run%20tests/badge.svg
:target: https://github.com/PyCQA/pydocstyle/actions?query=workflow%3A%22Run+tests%22+branch%3Amaster
```

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/pydocstyle/badge/?version=latest
:alt: Documentation Status
:target: https://readthedocs.org/projects/pydocstyle/?badge=latest
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/pydocstyle.svg
:target: https://pypi.org/project/pydocstyle
```

```{image} https://pepy.tech/badge/pydocstyle
:target: https://pepy.tech/project/pydocstyle
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg
:target: https://github.com/psf/black
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/badge/%20imports-isort-%231674b1?style=flat&labelColor=ef8336
:target: https://pycqa.github.io/isort/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/badge/Gitpod-ready--to--code-blue?logo=gitpod
:alt: Gitpod ready-to-code
:target: https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/PyCQA/pydocstyle
```

**pydocstyle** is a static analysis tool for checking compliance with Python
docstring conventions.

**pydocstyle** supports most of
[PEP 257](http://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0257/) out of the box, but it
should not be considered a reference implementation.

**pydocstyle** supports Python 3.6+.

## Quick Start

### Install

```
pip install pydocstyle
```

### Run

```
$ pydocstyle test.py
test.py:18 in private nested class `meta`:
        D101: Docstring missing
test.py:27 in public function `get_user`:
    D300: Use """triple double quotes""" (found '''-quotes)
test:75 in public function `init_database`:
    D201: No blank lines allowed before function docstring (found 1)
...
```

### Develop

You can use Gitpod to run pre-configured dev environment in the cloud right from your browser -

```{image} https://gitpod.io/button/open-in-gitpod.svg
:alt: Open in Gitpod
:target: https://gitpod.io/#https://github.com/PyCQA/pydocstyle
```

Before submitting a PR make sure that you run `make all`.

## Links

- [Read the full documentation here](https://pydocstyle.org/en/stable/).
- [Fork pydocstyle on GitHub](https://github.com/PyCQA/pydocstyle).
- [PyPI project page](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pydocstyle).

modules.name: pydocstyle.utils
documentation: General shared utilities.

functions.name: pydocstyle.utils.is_blank
documentation: Return True iff the string contains only whitespaces.

functions.name: pydocstyle.utils.pairwise
documentation:
Return pairs of items from `iterable`.

pairwise([1, 2, 3], default_value=None) -> (1, 2) (2, 3), (3, None)

functions.name: pydocstyle.utils.common_prefix_length
documentation:
Return the length of the longest common prefix of a and b.

```pycon
>>> common_prefix_length('abcd', 'abce')
3
```

functions.name: pydocstyle.utils.strip_non_alphanumeric
documentation: Strip string from any non-alphanumeric characters.

modules.name: pydocstyle.wordlists
documentation:
Wordlists loaded from package data.

We can treat them as part of the code for the imperative mood check, and
therefore we load them at import time, rather than on-demand.

functions.name: pydocstyle.wordlists.load_wordlist
documentation:
Iterate over lines of a wordlist data file.

`name` should be the name of a package data file within the data/
directory.

Whitespace and #-prefixed comments are stripped from each line.

functions.name: pydocstyle.wordlists.make_imperative_verbs_dict
documentation: Create a dictionary mapping stemmed verbs to the imperative form.

Initialize the object.

`parameters` are specific to the created error.

methods.name: pydocstyle.violations.Error.message
documentation: Return the message to print to the user.

methods.name: pydocstyle.violations.Error.lines
documentation: Return the source code lines for this error.
Error in docstring style.

methods.name: pydocstyle.violations.ErrorRegistry.to_rst

documentation: type: ignore

inner_classes.name: pydocstyle.violations.ErrorRegistry.ErrorGroup

methods.name: pydocstyle.violations.ErrorRegistry.ErrorGroup.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the object.

`Prefix` should be the common prefix for errors in this group,
e.g., "D1".
`name` is the name of the group (its subject).

methods.name: pydocstyle.violations.ErrorRegistry.ErrorGroup.create_error
documentation: Create an error, register it to this group and return it.
A group of similarly themed errors.
A registry of all error codes, divided to groups.

functions.name: pydocstyle.cli.setup_stream_handlers

modules.name: pydocstyle.__main__
documentation:
Static analysis tool for checking docstring conventions and style.

The repository is located at:
http://github.com/PyCQA/pydocstyle
! /usr/bin/env python

modules.name: pydocstyle.config
documentation: Configuration file parsing and utilities.

functions.name: pydocstyle.config.check_initialized
documentation: Check that the configuration object was initialized.

variables.name: pydocstyle.config.tomllib
documentation: type: ignore

classes.name: pydocstyle.config.TomlParser

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.TomlParser.__init__
documentation: Create a toml parser.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.TomlParser.read
documentation:
Read and parse a filename or an iterable of filenames.

Files that cannot be opened are silently ignored; this is
designed so that you can specify an iterable of potential
configuration file locations (e.g. current directory, user's
home directory, systemwide directory), and all existing
configuration files in the iterable will be read.  A single
filename may also be given.

Return list of successfully read files.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.TomlParser.has_section
documentation: Indicate whether the named section is present in the configuration.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.TomlParser.options
documentation: Return a list of option names for the given section name.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.TomlParser.get
documentation: Get an option value for a given section.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.TomlParser.getboolean
documentation: Get a boolean option value for a given section.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.TomlParser.getint
documentation: Get an integer option value for a given section.

ConfigParser that partially mimics RawConfigParser but for toml files.

See RawConfigParser for more info. Also, please note that not all
RawConfigParser functionality is implemented, but only the subset that is
currently used by pydocstyle.

classes.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser.__init__
documentation: Create a configuration parser.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser.get_default_run_configuration
documentation: Return a `RunConfiguration` object set with default values.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser.parse
documentation:
Parse the configuration.

If one of `BASE_ERROR_SELECTION_OPTIONS` was selected, overrides all
error codes to check and disregards any error code related
configurations from the configuration files.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser.get_user_run_configuration
documentation: Return the run configuration for the script.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser.get_files_to_check
documentation:
Generate files and error codes to check on each one.

Walk dir trees under `self._arguments` and yield file names
that `match` under each directory that `match_dir`.
The method locates the configuration for each file name and yields a
tuple of (filename, [error_codes]).

With every discovery of a new configuration file `IllegalConfiguration`
might be raised.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser._get_config_by_discovery
documentation:
Get a configuration for checking `node` by config discovery.

Config discovery happens when no explicit config file is specified. The
file system is searched for config files starting from the directory
containing the file being checked, and up until the root directory of
the project.

See `_get_config` for further details.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser._get_config
documentation:
Get and cache the run configuration for `node`.

If no configuration exists (not local and not for the parent node),
returns and caches a default configuration.

The algorithm:
-------------
* If the current directory's configuration exists in
   `self._cache` - return it.
* If a configuration file does not exist in this directory:
  * If the directory is not a root directory:
    * Cache its configuration as this directory's and return it.
  * Else:
    * Cache a default configuration and return it.
* Else:
  * Read the configuration file.
  * If a parent directory exists AND the configuration file
    allows inheritance:
    * Read the parent configuration by calling this function with the
      parent directory as `node`.
    * Merge the parent configuration with the current one and
      cache it.
* If the user has specified one of `BASE_ERROR_SELECTION_OPTIONS` in
  the CLI - return the CLI configuration with the configuration match
  clauses
* Set the `--add-select` and `--add-ignore` CLI configurations.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser._get_node_dir
documentation: Return the absolute path of the directory of a filesystem node.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser._read_configuration_file
documentation:
Try to read and parse `path` as a configuration file.

If the configurations were illegal (checked with
`self._validate_options`), raises `IllegalConfiguration`.

Returns (options, should_inherit).

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser._merge_configuration
documentation:
Merge parent config into the child options.

The migration process requires an `options` object for the child in
order to distinguish between mutually exclusive codes, add-select and
add-ignore error codes.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser._parse_args
documentation: Parse the options using `self._parser` and reformat the options.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser._create_run_config
documentation: Create a `RunConfiguration` object from `options`.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser._create_check_config
documentation:
Create a `CheckConfiguration` object from `options`.

If `use_defaults`, any of the match options that are `None` will
be replaced with their default value and the default convention will be
set for the checked codes.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser._get_section_name
documentation: Parse options from relevant section.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser._get_config_file_in_folder
documentation:
Look for a configuration file in `path`.

If exists return its full path, otherwise None.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser._get_exclusive_error_codes
documentation: Extract the error codes from the selected exclusive option.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser._set_add_options
documentation: Set `checked_codes` by the `add_ignore` or `add_select` options.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser._expand_error_codes
documentation: Return an expanded set of error codes to ignore.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser._get_checked_errors
documentation: Extract the codes needed to be checked from `options`.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser._validate_options
documentation:
Validate the mutually exclusive options.

Return `True` iff only zero or one of `BASE_ERROR_SELECTION_OPTIONS`
was selected.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser._has_exclusive_option
documentation: Return `True` iff one or more exclusive options were selected.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser._fix_set_options
documentation: Alter the set options from None/strings to sets in place.

methods.name: pydocstyle.config.ConfigurationParser._create_option_parser
documentation: Return an option parser to parse the command line arguments.

Responsible for parsing configuration from files and CLI.

There are 2 types of configurations: Run configurations and Check
configurations.

Run Configurations:
------------------
Responsible for deciding things that are related to the user interface and
configuration discovery, e.g. verbosity, debug options, etc.
All run configurations default to `False` or `None` and are decided only
by CLI.

Check Configurations:
--------------------
Configurations that are related to which files and errors will be checked.
These are configurable in 2 ways: using the CLI, and using configuration
files.

Configuration files are nested within the file system, meaning that the
closer a configuration file is to a checked file, the more relevant it will
be. For instance, imagine this directory structure:

A
+-- tox.ini: sets `select=D100`
+-- B
    +-- foo.py
    +-- tox.ini: sets `add-ignore=D100`

Then `foo.py` will not be checked for `D100`.
The configuration build algorithm is described in `self._get_config`.

Note: If any of `BASE_ERROR_SELECTION_OPTIONS` was selected in the CLI, all
configuration files will be ignored and each file will be checked for
the error codes supplied in the CLI.

classes.name: pydocstyle.config.IllegalConfiguration
documentation: An exception for illegal configurations.

modules.name: pydocstyle.checker
documentation: Parsed source code checkers for docstring violations.

functions.name: pydocstyle.checker.check
documentation:
Generate docstring errors that exist in `filenames` iterable.

By default, the PEP-257 convention is checked. To specifically define the
set of error codes to check for, supply either `select` or `ignore` (but
not both). In either case, the parameter should be a collection of error
code strings, e.g., {'D100', 'D404'}.

When supplying `select`, only specified error codes will be reported.
When supplying `ignore`, all error codes which were not specified will be
reported.

Note that ignored error code refer to the entire set of possible
error codes, which is larger than just the PEP-257 convention. To your
convenience, you may use `pydocstyle.violations.conventions.pep257` as
a base set to add or remove errors from.

`ignore_inline_noqa` controls if `# noqa` comments are respected or not.

`ignore_self_only_init` controls if D107 is reported on \_\_init\_\_ only containing `self`.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> check(['pydocstyle.py'])
<generator object check at 0x...>
```

```pycon
>>> check(['pydocstyle.py'], select=['D100'])
<generator object check at 0x...>
```

```pycon
>>> check(['pydocstyle.py'], ignore=conventions.pep257 - {'D100'})
<generator object check at 0x...>
```

functions.name: pydocstyle.checker.is_ascii
documentation: Return a boolean indicating if `string` only has ascii characters.

functions.name: pydocstyle.checker.leading_space
documentation: Return any leading space from `string`.

functions.name: pydocstyle.checker.get_leading_words
documentation:
Return any leading set of words from `line`.

For example, if `line` is "  Hello world!!!", returns "Hello world".

functions.name: pydocstyle.checker.is_def_arg_private
documentation: Return a boolean indicating if the argument name is private.

functions.name: pydocstyle.checker.get_function_args
documentation: Return the function arguments given the source-code string.

classes.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker.check_docstring_missing
documentation:
D10{0,1,2,3}: Public definitions should have docstrings.

All modules should normally have docstrings.  [...] all functions and
classes exported by a module should also have docstrings. Public
methods (including the __init__ constructor) should also have
docstrings.

Note: Public (exported) definitions are either those with names listed
      in __all__ variable (if present), or those that do not start
      with a single underscore.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker.check_docstring_empty
documentation:
D419: Docstring is empty.

If the user provided a docstring but it was empty, it is like they never provided one.

NOTE: This used to report as D10X errors.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker.check_one_liners
documentation:
D200: One-liner docstrings should fit on one line with quotes.

The closing quotes are on the same line as the opening quotes.
This looks better for one-liners.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker.check_no_blank_before
documentation:
D20{1,2}: No blank lines allowed around function/method docstring.

There's no blank line either before or after the docstring unless directly
followed by an inner function or class.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker.check_blank_before_after_class
documentation:
D20{3,4}: Class docstring should have 1 blank line around them.

Insert a blank line before and after all docstrings (one-line or
multi-line) that document a class -- generally speaking, the class's
methods are separated from each other by a single blank line, and the
docstring needs to be offset from the first method by a blank line;
for symmetry, put a blank line between the class header and the
docstring.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker.check_blank_after_summary
documentation:
D205: Put one blank line between summary line and description.

Multi-line docstrings consist of a summary line just like a one-line
docstring, followed by a blank line, followed by a more elaborate
description. The summary line may be used by automatic indexing tools;
it is important that it fits on one line and is separated from the
rest of the docstring by a blank line.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker._get_docstring_indent
documentation: Return the indentation of the docstring's opening quotes.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker.check_indent
documentation:
D20{6,7,8}: The entire docstring should be indented same as code.

The entire docstring is indented the same as the quotes at its
first line.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker.check_newline_after_last_paragraph
documentation:
D209: Put multi-line docstring closing quotes on separate line.

Unless the entire docstring fits on a line, place the closing
quotes on a line by themselves.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker.check_surrounding_whitespaces
documentation: D210: No whitespaces allowed surrounding docstring text.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker.check_multi_line_summary_start
documentation:
D21{2,3}: Multi-line docstring summary style check.

A multi-line docstring summary should start either at the first,
or separately at the second line of a docstring.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker.check_triple_double_quotes
documentation:
D300: Use """triple double quotes""".

For consistency, always use """triple double quotes""" around
docstrings. Use r"""raw triple double quotes""" if you use any
backslashes in your docstrings. For Unicode docstrings, use
u"""Unicode triple-quoted strings""".

Note: Exception to this is made if the docstring contains
      """ quotes in its body.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker.check_backslashes
documentation:
D301: Use r""" if any backslashes in a docstring.

Use r"""raw triple double quotes""" if you use any backslashes
(\) in your docstrings.

Exceptions are backslashes for line-continuation and unicode escape
sequences \N... and \u... These are considered intended unescaped
content in docstrings.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker._check_ends_with
documentation:
First line ends with one of `chars`.

First line of the docstring should end with one of the characters in `chars`.
`chars` supports either a `str` or an `Iterable[str]`. If the condition is
evaluated to be false, it raises `violation`.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker.check_ends_with_period
documentation:
D400: First line should end with a period.

The [first line of a] docstring is a phrase ending in a period.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker.check_ends_with_punctuation
documentation:
D415: should end with proper punctuation.

The [first line of a] docstring is a phrase ending in a period,
question mark, or exclamation point

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker.check_imperative_mood
documentation:
D401: First line should be in imperative mood: 'Do', not 'Does'.

[Docstring] prescribes the function or method's effect as a command:
("Do this", "Return that"), not as a description; e.g. don't write
"Returns the pathname ...".

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker.check_no_signature
documentation:
D402: First line should not be function's or method's "signature".

The one-line docstring should NOT be a "signature" reiterating the
function/method parameters (which can be obtained by introspection).

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker.check_capitalized
documentation:
D403: First word of the first line should be properly capitalized.

The [first line of a] docstring is a phrase ending in a period.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker.check_if_needed
documentation:
D418: Function decorated with @overload shouldn't contain a docstring.

Functions that are decorated with @overload are definitions,
and are for the benefit of the type checker only,
since they will be overwritten by the non-@overload-decorated definition.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker.check_starts_with_this
documentation:
D404: First word of the docstring should not be `This`.

Docstrings should use short, simple language. They should not begin
with "This class is [..]" or "This module contains [..]".

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker._is_docstring_section
documentation:
Check if the suspected context is really a section header.

Lets have a look at the following example docstring:
    '''Title.

    Some part of the docstring that specifies what the function
    returns. <----- Not a real section name. It has a suffix and the
                    previous line is not empty and does not end with
                    a punctuation sign.

    This is another line in the docstring. It describes stuff,
    but we forgot to add a blank line between it and the section name.
    Parameters  <-- A real section name. The previous line ends with
    ----------      a period, therefore it is in a new
                    grammatical context.
    param : int
    examples : list  <------- Not a section - previous line doesn't end
        A list of examples.   with punctuation.
    notes : list  <---------- Not a section - there's text after the
        A list of notes.      colon.

    Notes:  <--- Suspected as a context because there's a suffix to the
    -----        section, but it's a colon so it's probably a mistake.
    Bla.

    '''

To make sure this is really a section we check these conditions:
    * There's no suffix to the section name or it's just a colon AND
    * The previous line is empty OR it ends with punctuation.

If one of the conditions is true, we will consider the line as
a section name.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker._check_blanks_and_section_underline
documentation:
D4{07,08,09,12,14}, D215: Section underline checks.

Check for correct formatting for docstring sections. Checks that:
    * The line that follows the section name contains
      dashes (D40{7,8}).
    * The amount of dashes is equal to the length of the section
      name (D409).
    * The section's content does not begin in the line that follows
      the section header (D412).
    * The section has no content (D414).
    * The indentation of the dashed line is equal to the docstring's
      indentation (D215).

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker._check_common_section
documentation:
D4{05,10,11,13}, D214: Section name checks.

Check for valid section names. Checks that:
    * The section name is properly capitalized (D405).
    * The section is not over-indented (D214).
    * There's a blank line after the section (D410, D413).
    * There's a blank line before the section (D411).

Also yields all the errors from `_check_blanks_and_section_underline`.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker._check_numpy_section
documentation:
D406: NumPy-style section name checks.

Check for valid section names. Checks that:
    * The section name has no superfluous suffix to it (D406).

Additionally, also yield all violations from `_check_common_section`
which are style-agnostic section checks.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker._check_parameters_section
documentation:
D417: `Parameters` section check for numpy style.

Check for a valid `Parameters` section. Checks that:
    * The section documents all function arguments (D417)
        except `self` or `cls` if it is a method.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker._check_args_section
documentation:
D417: `Args` section checks.

Check for a valid `Args` or `Argument` section. Checks that:
: - The section documents all function arguments (D417)
    : except `self` or `cls` if it is a method.

Documentation for each arg should start at the same indentation
level. For example, in this case x and y are distinguishable:

```
Args:
    x: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet
    y: Ut enim ad minim veniam
```

In the case below, we only recognize x as a documented parameter
because the rest of the content is indented as if it belongs
to the description for x:

```
Args:
    x: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet
        y: Ut enim ad minim veniam
```

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker._check_missing_args
documentation:
D417: Yield error for missing arguments in docstring.

Given a list of arguments found in the docstring and the
callable definition, it checks if all the arguments of the
callable are present in the docstring, else it yields a
D417 with a list of missing arguments.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker._check_google_section
documentation:
D416: Google-style section name checks.

Check for valid section names. Checks that:
    * The section does not contain any blank line between its name
      and content (D412).
    * The section is not empty (D414).
    * The section name has colon as a suffix (D416).

Additionally, also yield all violations from `_check_common_section`
which are style-agnostic section checks.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker._get_section_contexts
documentation:
Generate `SectionContext` objects for valid sections.

Given a list of `valid_section_names`, generate an
`Iterable[SectionContext]` which provides:
    * Section Name
    * String value of the previous line
    * The section line
    * Following lines till the next section
    * Line index of the beginning of the section in the docstring
    * Boolean indicating whether the section is the last section.
for each valid section.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker._check_numpy_sections
documentation:
NumPy-style docstring sections checks.

Check the general format of a sectioned docstring:
    '''This is my one-liner.

    Short Summary
    -------------
    This is my summary.

    Returns
    -------
    None.

    '''

Section names appear in `NUMPY_SECTION_NAMES`.
Yields all violation from `_check_numpy_section` for each valid
Numpy-style section.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker._check_google_sections
documentation:
Google-style docstring section checks.

Check the general format of a sectioned docstring:
    '''This is my one-liner.

    Note:
        This is my summary.

    Returns:
        None.

    '''

Section names appear in `GOOGLE_SECTION_NAMES`.
Yields all violation from `_check_google_section` for each valid
Google-style section.

methods.name: pydocstyle.checker.ConventionChecker.check_docstring_sections
documentation: Check for docstring sections.

Checker for PEP 257, NumPy and Google conventions.

D10x: Missing docstrings
D20x: Whitespace issues
D30x: Docstring formatting
D40x: Docstring content issues

modules.name: pydocstyle.parser
documentation: Python code parser.

classes.name: pydocstyle.parser.ParseError
documentation: An error parsing contents of a Python file.

classes.name: pydocstyle.parser.Value
documentation: A generic object with a list of preset fields.

classes.name: pydocstyle.parser.Definition

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Definition.error_lineno
documentation: Get the line number with which to report violations.

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Definition.source
documentation: Return the source code for the definition.

class_variables.name: pydocstyle.parser.Definition._fields
documentation: type: Tuple[str, ...]
A Python source code definition (could be class, function, etc).

classes.name: pydocstyle.parser.Module

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Module.is_public
documentation:
Return True iff the module is considered public.

This helps determine if it requires a docstring.

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Module._is_inside_private_package
documentation: Return True if the module is inside a private package.

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Module._is_public_name
documentation: Determine whether a "module name" (i.e. module or package name) is public.

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Module._is_private_name
documentation: Determine whether a "module name" (i.e. module or package name) is private.
A Python source code module.

classes.name: pydocstyle.parser.Package
documentation: A package is a __init__.py module.

classes.name: pydocstyle.parser.Function

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Function.is_public
documentation: Return True iff this function should be considered public.

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Function.is_overload
documentation: Return True iff the method decorated with overload.

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Function.is_property
documentation: Return True if the method is decorated with any property decorator.

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Function.is_test
documentation:
Return True if this function is a test function/method.

We exclude tests from the imperative mood check, because to phrase
their docstring in the imperative mood, they would have to start with
a highly redundant "Test that ...".

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Function.param_names
documentation: Return the parameter names.
A Python source code function.

classes.name: pydocstyle.parser.NestedFunction
documentation: A Python source code nested function.

classes.name: pydocstyle.parser.Method

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Method.is_magic
documentation: Return True iff this method is a magic method (e.g., `__str__`).

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Method.is_init
documentation: Return True iff this method is `__init__`.

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Method.is_public
documentation: Return True iff this method should be considered public.

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Method.is_static
documentation: Return True iff the method is static.
A Python source code method.

classes.name: pydocstyle.parser.Class
documentation: A Python source code class.

classes.name: pydocstyle.parser.NestedClass

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.NestedClass.is_public
documentation: Return True iff this class should be considered public.
A Python source code nested class.

classes.name: pydocstyle.parser.Decorator
documentation: A decorator for function, method or class.

classes.name: pydocstyle.parser.Docstring
documentation:
Represent a docstring.

This is a string, but has additional start/end attributes representing
the start and end of the token.

classes.name: pydocstyle.parser.AllError

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.AllError.__init__
documentation: Initialize the error with a more specific message.
Raised when there is a problem with __all__ when parsing.

classes.name: pydocstyle.parser.Parser

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Parser.parse
documentation: Parse the given file-like object and return its Module object.

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Parser.__call__
documentation: Call the parse method.
TODO: remove

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Parser.consume
documentation: Consume one token and verify it is of the expected kind.

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Parser.leapfrog
documentation:
Skip tokens in the stream until a certain token kind is reached.

If `value` is specified, tokens whose values are different will also
be skipped.

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Parser.parse_docstring
documentation: Parse a single docstring and return its value.

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Parser.parse_decorators
documentation:
Parse decorators into self._accumulated_decorators.

Called after first @ is found.
Continue to do so until encountering the 'def' or 'class' start token.

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Parser.parse_definitions
documentation: Parse multiple definitions and yield them.

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Parser.parse_dunder_all
documentation: Parse the __all__ definition in a module.

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Parser.parse_module
documentation: Parse a module (and its children) and return a Module object.

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Parser.parse_definition
documentation: Parse a definition and return its value in a `class_` object.

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Parser.parse_skip_comment
documentation: Parse a definition comment for noqa skips.

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Parser.check_current
documentation: Verify the current token is of type `kind` and equals `value`.

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Parser.parse_from_import_statement
documentation:
Parse a 'from x import y' statement.

The purpose is to find __future__ statements.

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Parser._parse_from_import_source
documentation:
Parse the 'from x import' part in a 'from x import y' statement.

Return true iff `x` is __future__.

methods.name: pydocstyle.parser.Parser._parse_from_import_names
documentation: Parse the 'y' part in a 'from x import y' statement.
A Python source code parser.
